home.name: Tomasz Skorupski
home.lead:
I build websites and web applications and provide IT support and administration. I combine the skills of a developer and an IT specialist, which allows me to deliver solutions that are not only functional, but also stable and secure.

I am open to long-term cooperation with a company where I can bring real value from day one and grow in a stable environment. I also take on individual projects for businesses and individuals - you can find their scope in the Services section.
home.caption: The technological foundation of your business

about.title: Bio
about.p1: Hi! I’m Tomasz Skorupski. For years, I ensured that IT systems ran smoothly by managing servers (Windows/Linux), networks, and databases. However, I realized that I wanted to do more than just fix and maintain things-I wanted to create them. Today, I use my solid technical background to build and improve digital solutions.
about.videoInvite: If you prefer listening over reading – feel free to watch my short video introduction
about.approachIntro: I’m not just a developer, nor just an administrator. I am a versatile specialist who sees the bigger picture. My hybrid expertise ensures that the solutions I design are not only innovative but also infrastructurally robust.

points.design: Design & Improve: I write code and build applications while taking care of their architecture, testing, and optimization to eliminate defects.
points.deploy: Deploy: I use Git and Docker and develop core CI/CD skills so that my workflows are repeatable and safe.
points.support: Support & Understand: Thanks to experience in IT Support and Administration, I understand the user perspective and operational challenges. I know how crucial stability and security are in day-to-day work.

about.growth: In parallel, I’m developing my competencies by delivering my own projects. While professionally I focus on your needs, privately I’m passionate about the development and practical use of AI agents in automation - which always translates into a better understanding of modern architectures.
about.goal: I’m not just looking for "another gig". Above all, I’m seeking long-term collaboration with a Partner or Employer with whom I can build a relationship for years - a place where I can bring real value from day one and grow through ambitious projects. That said, I’m open to freelance work too, especially if it can be a stepping stone to something more.

facts.locationValue: Stargard/Szczecin, Poland • open to remote work/hybrid
facts.statusValue: Actively seeking a stable, long-term role
facts.educationValue: The West Pomeranian Business School - IT - Mobile Applications
facts.cert4Name: Use of UML in the field of geographic information (Navoica, 2026)
facts.cert6Name: Hardware Configuration and Computer Network Administration (Navoica, 2026)

responsibility.name: Responsibility
responsibility.desc: I deliver tasks on time and as agreed.

passion.name: Passion
passion.desc: I constantly develop and raise quality in projects.

teamwork.name: Teamwork
teamwork.desc: I share knowledge and work with the team toward a common goal.

independence.name: Independence
independence.desc: I analyze problems and implement solutions independently, without waiting for instructions.

creativity.name: Creativity
creativity.desc: I propose alternatives and turn them into concrete features.

flexibility.name: Flexibility
flexibility.desc: I quickly adapt to changing requirements and new environments.

bullets: Comprehensive management of the IT environment, including administration of systems, servers and databases, as well as their development and modernization - with a focus on stability, security and the real needs of everyday users.

Resolving employees’ technical and functional issues based on collaboration, analysis and understanding how technology affects their daily work - from minor hiccups to complex incidents requiring deeper diagnostics.

Creating and developing content and functionality in internal systems, including documentation, modules and enhancements that improved communication, streamlined processes and increased efficiency across the company.

Participating in automation and process-optimization initiatives by designing tools and solutions that eliminated repetitive tasks, improved system reliability and noticeably enhanced user experience.

Deployment and integration of secure/pull printing systems for clients across diverse industries - from production floors to accounting offices and notary chambers - tailored to their workflows, processes and real business needs.
Resolving technical issues remotely and on-site through direct communication, process analysis and rapid adaptation of solutions that restored the continuity of entire teams’ work.
Optimization and stabilization of printing environments, including streamlining device communication, improving throughput and reducing latency affecting key operational processes.
Ongoing supervision of devices and printing environments, proactive monitoring and rapid response to anomalies so that technology remained a safe, predictable work tool for clients.

Administration of systems and network infrastructure, including rolling out new technologies, modernizing the environment, and ensuring the stability and security of tools used across the organization at every level.

Building a new server room from the ground up: physical assembly, configuration and commissioning of network and server hardware - a project requiring precision, planning and responsibility for the foundations of the company’s entire infrastructure.
Ensuring uninterrupted operation of key applications and services - from operational tools to end-user systems - with quick incident response and a strong focus on minimizing downtime.
Close cooperation with users and teams, focused on rapidly resolving tickets, explaining how systems work and fostering an environment where technology genuinely improved daily work.
Configuration and supervision of IT systems and administration of network infrastructure supporting the institution’s stable operations and secure flow of data in day-to-day team work.
Ongoing user support in daily tasks, swift response to tickets and resolution of technical issues - helping turn technology into a tool rather than an obstacle for employees.
Ensuring continuous availability of systems and services, monitoring their performance and promptly eliminating irregularities to minimize downtime and keep core operational processes efficient.
Collaboration with various departments to better understand their needs and adapt IT solutions to how they actually work.
IT support for retail: service tickets, diagnostics and repair of POS equipment.
Network and device configuration, IT assets inventory.
Fiscal devices service - solving diverse issues across many locations.
Service and configuration of printing devices, user hardware support in bank branches.
On-site diagnostics and work continuity restoration.
Preparing, configuring and deploying new equipment in the field.
Remote IT support for hospitals and emergency medical teams.
Handling tickets: diagnostics, incident resolution, escalation.
Configurations and changes: workstations, medical apps, permissions.

services.title: IT Services
services.intro: I build websites and web applications and provide IT support for individuals and small businesses. I combine the roles of developer and systems administrator - so you get a solution that not only looks great, but is also stable, secure, and ready to go. I work remotely (from the Szczecin/Stargard area), with direct, straightforward communication - no middlemen, no agency markups.

card1.title: Website creation for businesses and individuals
card1.desc: I design and build modern websites: business cards, company sites, landing pages, and portfolios. Each one is responsive, fast, and optimised for basic SEO so your clients can find you on Google. I can build on a custom CMS or WordPress - with a panel where you can update content yourself, no coding required.
card1.forWhom: small businesses, freelancers, local companies, individuals.

card2.title: Custom web applications and process automation
card2.desc: I build applications tailored to specific needs: admin panels, simple CRM systems, management tools, and apps that automate repetitive tasks. I can also integrate with external services via API - e.g. payments, e-invoicing (KSeF), or AI tools. If you're running your business in Excel spreadsheets, I can turn that into a proper application.
card2.forWhom: businesses looking to streamline and automate their work.

card3.title: Programming help, fixes, and extensions
card3.desc: I also handle smaller programming jobs - no need to commission a full project. I'll fix and extend an existing site or app, squash bugs, write a script to automate tedious repetitive tasks, or help finish a project that got stuck. A great option when you need specific, fast technical help.
card3.forWhom: individuals and businesses with ongoing, smaller programming needs.

card4.title: IT support and outsourcing for small businesses
card4.desc: I'll handle the technical side of your business so you can focus on running it. I offer server administration (Windows and Linux), network and device configuration, backups, deployments, and user support. In practice, I act as an external IT department for a small company - available remotely, without the cost of hiring a full-time IT person.
card4.forWhom: small businesses without their own IT department.

step1.title: Discovery call
step1.desc: You tell me what you need; we define the scope together. Free of charge.

step2.title: Quote & plan
step2.desc: You receive a clear quote and estimated timeline, with no hidden costs.

step3.title: Delivery
step3.desc: I build the solution; we stay in constant contact.

step4.title: Launch & support
step4.desc: I launch the project and remain available for support afterwards.

why1.title: Developer and sysadmin in one
why1.desc: I build solutions that are not only functional, but also stable and secure - because I understand the full infrastructure they run on.

why2.title: Direct contact
why2.desc: You talk to the person doing the work, not a middleman.

why3.title: Live portfolio
why3.desc: You can see my projects live (live demo), not just in screenshots.

why4.title: Individual approach
why4.desc: I tailor the solution to your needs and budget, not the other way around.

services.ctaTitle: Got an idea or a problem to solve?
services.ctaAccent: the quote and first conversation are free.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.subtitleHtml:
As most of my past projects were developed for employers and remain subject to sharing restrictions. At the same time, I am creating a series of my own demo projects - new items will appear here soon.

Note: I use free‑tier backend services on Render in some projects. It is recommended to wake up the services using Shellty Pulse before using the application.

testAccount.title: Test account
testAccount.note: Log in to see the application features
testAccount.noteKsef: Use the following data to test the integration with KSeF

shelltyCMS.title: Shellty CMS
shelltyCMS.subtitle: - WordPress Alternative for B2B -
shelltyCMS.description: Custom CMS on Next.js and Payload as a lightweight alternative to WordPress. Full control over code and content without unnecessary baggage.
highlights: 🔄 Clone & Deploy: 12 ready-made collections, replace content in CMS instead of writing code from scratch
🌍 i18n PL/EN: Payload localization, [locale] routing and URL helpers out of the box
🖼️ Cloudinary Plugin: Automatic upload and auto-delete of media on free tier
🔒 Security: Upstash rate limit, SHA-256 IP hashing, CSRF and honeypot
🤖 AI Lead Agent (WIP): Chat widget based on Gemini 2.5 Flash and Vercel AI SDK

shelltyPulse.title: Shellty Pulse - Service Health Monitor & Keep-Alive
shelltyPulse.subtitle: Service Health Monitor & Keep-Alive.
shelltyPulse.description: A professional-grade Service Health Monitor designed for real-time tracking of web service availability. It acts as an intelligent "keep-alive" mechanism for applications on free-tier platforms (e.g., Render), preventing idle sleep through automated, configurable pings.
highlights: Infrastructure as code & containerization: combining Docker Compose with Ansible for fully automated provisioning and consistent, repeatable server-side deployments.
End-to-end CI/CD automation: a robust GitHub Actions pipeline covering image builds, API endpoint testing, and automated deployment to ensure code reliability with every push.

postlio.title: Postlio (in development)
postlio.subtitle: - AI Social Media Autopilot -
postlio.description: A modern PWA application for comprehensive Social Media management with AI support. Enables creating, scheduling, and fully autonomous publishing of content on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn.
highlights: 🤖 Autopilot Mode: Autonomous post generation and queuing system based on the brand's unique "Voice DNA", running in the background via APScheduler
🎨 AI Creator: Hybrid text (Gemini/Groq), image (Flux/SDXL), and video engine with an intelligent automatic provider switching mechanism (fallback)
🔒 Data Protection: Secure storage of OAuth tokens encrypted symmetrically (Fernet AES) and asynchronous backend architecture in FastAPI 0.110
📲 PWA & Liquid Design: Responsive Next.js 14 interface with glass-morphism effect, fully adapted for smartphone installation and offline use

smartQuoteAI.title: SmartQuote
smartQuoteAI.subtitle: - CRM & Sales Automation -
smartQuoteAI.description: CRM with AI quote generation, PDF block editor, and e-invoice integration. Production-deployed.
highlights:
🤖  AI (Gemini 2.5 Flash)
Content generation for quotes, profitability analysis, post-mortem

📄  Dual PDF Engine
PDFKit (classic) + Puppeteer/Vercel (proposal)

✍️  E-signature with Audit
Canvas + SHA-256 hash, token-based public pages

🔗  KSeF Integration
HMAC-SHA256 webhook, idempotent send, NIP cache

shelltyKanban.title: Shellty.Kanban
shelltyKanban.subtitle: - Real-time Project Management -
shelltyKanban.description: A fullstack Kanban application for task management and team collaboration, built from scratch with .NET 10 and React 18. It includes board and card management with drag & drop, real-time collaboration via SignalR, and a complete Web Push system that works even when the app is closed.
shelltyKanban.highlightsTitle: WHAT MAKES THIS PROJECT STAND OUT:
highlights: 📡 Real-time with SignalR: live board updates, presence indicators, and "who is editing" status visible to all users without page refresh.
🔔 Web Push (VAPID + Service Worker): granular notification preferences per device and event type (create, update, move, delete, assign); system notifications work even when the app is closed.
🔒 JWT + Refresh Token: token rotation on every use, interceptor-based auto-refresh; secure per-device logout without disabling push notifications on other devices.
⚙️ CI/CD + Tests: GitHub Actions builds, tests, and deploys; 60+ xUnit tests with code coverage above 95%.

shelltyBlog.title: Shellty Blog
shelltyBlog.subtitle: - Modern MVC Blog Platform -
shelltyBlog.description: A modern CMS system built on ASP.NET Core MVC architecture with emphasis on design patterns and Clean Code. Fully containerized application integrated with a cloud PostgreSQL database (Neon).
highlights: Democratic Roles: Unique permission system requiring unanimous voting from all active administrators
MVC Architecture: Clean code structure based on file-scoped namespaces, nullable reference types, and zero compiler warnings
Image Handling: Advanced image upload module with server-side size and format validation and client-side preview
Dockerized: Full containerization ensuring stable deployment on Render using lightweight Linux x64 images

mobiSalon.title: Grooming Business Card with CMS
mobiSalon.subtitle: Website for a grooming salon.
mobiSalon.description: This is a light and clear one-page website designed with Next.js/React. The project combines an aesthetic presentation of a grooming salon with functionality that facilitates daily communication with customers. The site is fully responsive, which guarantees its impeccable operation and readability on smartphones and tablets.
mobiSalon.highlightsTitle: The heart of the site management is the News section, over which the owner has full control from the CMS panel (Decap CMS). They can instantly publish there:
highlights: Current announcements: important information about promotions, changes in the offer, or announcements for customers.
Availability calendar: an interactive summary where the administrator marks working days and free slots, informing customers about the salon's current schedule in real time.

pwaCookbook.title: Mobile Cookbook (PWA)
pwaCookbook.subtitle: Progressive Web Application - diploma project.
pwaCookbook.description: I developed a progressive web application (PWA) that helps in planning meals based on ingredients available in the kitchen. It integrates a recipe database, preparation instructions, and side dish cooking times, and a built-in timer with sound notification facilitates process control. The application was designed with a mobile-first approach and also works offline, remaining available on any device with a browser. As a result, it realy shortens the time spent on cooking and facilitates decision-making "what to cook". Project implemented based on React with an emphasis on performance and user convenience. It was created as part of a diploma thesis.

animalsOnePage.title: Interesting Animals - One Page
animalsOnePage.subtitle: Simple One Page website (student project).
animalsOnePage.description: A very simple, static one-page website prepared during studies as part of one of the tasks. It presents selected, interesting animal species in short sections with basic information and trivia. I treat it as a modest supplementary material showing consistency in documenting progress. I am aware that this is not a project representative of my current skills and I will soon replace it with newer, original projects.

ksefMaster.title: KSeF Master
ksefMaster.subtitle: - National E-Invoicing Ecosystem -
ksefMaster.description: Professional platform for integration with the government e-invoicing system (KSeF). .NET 8 and React 19 architecture providing full support for FA(2) documents, XML schema validation, and PDF visualizations.
highlights: API v2 Integration: Full communication with the Ministry of Finance gateway, online/offline session handling, and authorization via tokens and certificates
FA(2) Compliance: Generation of validatable XML documents and instant PDF visualizations with QR codes using the QuestPDF engine
Monorepo Workflow: Unified lifecycle of React 19 frontend and ASP.NET Core 8 backend in a single repository with automated CI/CD
Enterprise Architecture: Use of EF Core 8, FluentValidation, and JWT to ensure the highest data quality and transaction security

budmaxTheme.title: BudMax Theme
budmaxTheme.subtitle: Custom WordPress theme designed for the BudMax construction company. Minimalist design, performance, and full content control via ACF.
budmaxTheme.description: The project was created as a complete marketing solution - from PHP architecture through CSS/JS animations to AJAX forms with anti-spam protection. The theme does not use the block editor, which guarantees speed and code predictability.
highlights: Custom i18n system (PL/EN) based on cookies and query params - without WooCommerce Multilingual or WPML.
Speed Dial FAB with contact modal, honeypot, and rate-limiting.
Focal point (image cropping) controlled from the ACF panel via object-position.
SEO: hreflang, canonical, Open Graph - generated dynamically.
Deployment to Pantheon via Git with cache busting via filemtime().

tomiForno.title: Tomi Forno
tomiForno.subtitle: - Neapolitan Pizzeria · Full-Stack -
tomiForno.description: A multi-page full-stack pizzeria website built as a portfolio project. Features an interactive menu, gallery, reservation system, and a custom pizza builder. The builder includes an AI agent that suggests ingredient combinations, recommending which toppings pair well together.
tomiForno.highlightsTitle: WHAT MAKES THIS PROJECT STAND OUT:
highlights: 🍕 Pizza Builder: Interactive layer-by-layer composer (sauce/cheese/toppings) with live visual preview and hybrid pricing (dough + flat toppings)
🤖 AI Agent: Ingredient pairing suggestions based on flavour compatibility - the agent recommends which toppings work well together
🌍 i18n PL/EN: next-intl with [locale] routing, full bilingual support with language switcher in mobile navigation
📱 Mobile Nav: Pizza wheel as UX signature - hamburger opens a full-screen overlay with interactive navigator

contact.title: Contact form
contact.subtitle: Have a question or a collaboration idea? Fill out the form or email me directly.

privacyNote.text: By sending a message, you agree to the processing of your data in accordance with the

s1.title: 1. Data Controller
s1.p1: The controller of your personal data is Tomasz Skorupski (hereinafter: the Controller), owner of this website.

s2.title: 2. What data do I collect and for what purpose?
s2.intro: Through the contact form available on this website, I collect exclusively:
s2.purpose: This data is collected solely for the purpose of establishing contact and responding to the message submitted via the form.

s3.title: 3. Legal basis for processing
s3.p: Your data is processed on the basis of Article 6(1)(f) of the GDPR, i.e. the legitimate interest of the Controller, which is responding to your contact inquiry.

s4.title: 4. How long do I store your data?
s4.p: Your data is stored for the time necessary to handle your inquiry. After the correspondence is concluded, the data is not processed for any other purpose.

s5.title: 5. Data recipients – Formspree
s5.p1: To handle the contact form, I use the Formspree service (Formspree, Inc.), which acts as a data processor on my behalf. This means that messages submitted through the form are transmitted via Formspree servers.
s5.p2: Formspree is a company based in the United States, which means your data may be transferred outside the European Economic Area (EEA). Formspree ensures an adequate level of data protection in accordance with applicable regulations.
s5.linkLabel: More information can be found in Formspree's privacy policy:
s5.p3: Apart from that, your data is not shared with any other third parties.

s6.title: 6. Cookies and analytics tools
s6.ga: This website uses Google Analytics (Google LLC), which uses cookies to analyze website traffic. Data collected by Google Analytics is anonymous and does not allow direct identification of the user. Google LLC is based in the USA – more information:

s7.title: 7. Your rights
s7.intro: In connection with the processing of your personal data, you have the following rights:
rights: Right of access – you may ask what data I hold about you.
Right to rectification – you may request correction of inaccurate data.
Right to erasure – you may request deletion of your data (right to be forgotten).
Right to restriction of processing – you may request that processing of your data be suspended.
Right to object – you may object to processing based on legitimate interest.
Right to lodge a complaint – if you believe I am processing your data unlawfully, you may lodge a complaint with the relevant supervisory authority in your country.
s7.contact: To exercise the above rights, please contact me at:
